def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd; import numpy as np; import ta
    short_ma = df['close'].rolling(window=params['short_window']).mean()
    long_ma = df['close'].rolling(window=params['long_window']).mean()
    atr = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=params['atr_window']).average_true_range()
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[(short_ma > long_ma) & (df['close'] > df['close'].shift(1) + params['atr_multiplier'] * atr)] = 1
    signals[(short_ma < long_ma) & (df['close'] < df['close'].shift(1) - params['atr_multiplier'] * atr)] = -1
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/USD exhibits momentum breakout behavior when short-term moving average crosses long-term moving average, confirmed by volatility-adjusted thresholds.
